headline: Transcript: Congress’ healthcare fight carries big consequences
summary: Marc Filippino talks to Lauren Fedor and Guy Chazan
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Swamp Notes podcast episode: ‘Congress’ healthcare fight carries big consequences’

Marc FilippinoRemember the government shutdown from oh so long ago?News clipThe clock is ticking down to a vote in the House of Representatives that would bring it into the nation’s longest shutdown.

Marc FilippinoWell, there was one big reason why it happened. News clipAs it stands, people who don’t get insurance coverage through work, Medicare or Medicaid, will see their tax credits expire at the end of this year.

Marc FilippinoAnd that reason — healthcare — it never really went away. Now, Republicans in Congress are facing down the decision to spend billions on insurance subsidies or force lots of Americans to pay more than double their current health insurance bills.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

This is Swamp Notes, the weekly podcast from the Financial Times, where we talk about all the things happening in US politics, finance and the economy. I’m Marc Filippino, and this
